title: Wide Boundary News: The Iranian War, Rising Gas Prices, and the Single Point Failure
content: In this installment, Nate addresses the U.S. and Israeli military offensive against Iran and traces the reverberating effects that extend far beyond the conflict itself, starting with what the closure of the Strait of Hormuz means for a civilization that routes a massive share of its physical economy through a single maritime corridor.
creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Inspirational Collapses? Learning from the civilisations that tried to break down well
content: We believe that more historians and archaeologists can step into a public, reflective role, helping societies explore narratives beyond either progress and growth fetishism on the one hand, or nostalgic and fortress mentalities on the other.
creator: Jem Bendell
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Crazy Town: Episode 120. You Ain’t Gonna Live Forever: The Dos and Don’ts of Legacy Building
content: In this episode, we run a special fantasy-football style draft to take a look at immortality projects, some horrendous, but some with positive effects.
creator: Asher Miller
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Crazy Town: Episode 120. You Ain’t Gonna Live Forever: The Dos and Don’ts of Legacy Building
content: In this episode, we run a special fantasy-football style draft to take a look at immortality projects, some horrendous, but some with positive effects.
creator: Asher Miller
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: When Experts Go Silent: Climate Misinformation Threatens Rights
content: The lesson shown by the LA wildfires is clear. When misinformation turns experts into targets of distrust and attack and weakens their public speech, the public is ultimately pushed further away from life-saving information at the moment they are most vulnerable.
creator: Taekyung Hwang
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Federico Savini on Degrowth and Its Future
content: But to the extent that economic growth and carbon-fuel usage are tightly linked – and not capable of being “decoupled” – some degrowth agenda is ultimately inescapable. The only question may be whether it will arrive via catastrophe or choice.
creator: David Bollier
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Infrastructure, finance, and the sale of the future
content: Mitchell’s new book reinforces this essential point. We must see through the alibi of capital, we must reclaim society from the grip of the economy, and we must change the rules of the game to save our collective future.
creator: Bart Hawkins Kreps
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: A Guide to Staying Human (Part 1): Desperately Seeking Agency
content: In this week’s Frankly, Nate begins a new series called “Staying Human,” which focuses on what he sees as a precondition for everything else: recovering a sense of personal agency.
creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Iran war: What we’re in for and why logic is your friend
content: A lot of nonsense is being communicated about the war with Iran.
creator: Kurt Cobb
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Do Beavers Care About Climate Justice?
content: By moving from seeing nature as something we - a distinct group causing harm - need to protect, to understanding it as a system we are actively involved in, battling climate change becomes less a concept of preservation and more a question of how we can help to shape a world that allows many beings to thrive.
creator: Evelyn Byrne
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The Future of Forests
content: Human communities have benefitted immensely from trees, but tree communities (i.e., forests) haven’t always fared so well in the bargain. What can we do differently to ensure a forested future?
creator: Richard Heinberg
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Human Nature Odyssey: Episode 20: Joanna Macy & The Great Turning — with Jess Serrante
content: Legendary activist Joanna Macy called this moment the Great Unraveling—a time when our ecological, political, economic, and social systems crumble. And yet, she also insisted that we stand on the threshold of a Great Turning: a profound transition toward a more just and sustainable world.
creator: Alex Leff
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.
